english: hence .p'rén-ba ('phreng ba) esp.: a string of beads, rosary; bgrań-.p'reń (bgrang 'phreng), rosary for counting the repetitions of prayers and magic spells, being used also in arithmetic, as an aid to memory; mú tig-.p'reń (mu tig 'phreng) string of pearls, rosary composed of pearls; nor-bu-.p'reń-ba (nor bu 'phreng ba) of precious stones; also title of a book; fig. don ma go tsíg-gi .p'rén-ba bzuń (don ma go tshig gi 'phreng ba bzung), they only keep to the string of words, without understanding their import Mil.

vb. n. to love, to be fond of, greatly attached to, with accus. of the person, séms-la (sems la) and similar supplementary words being generally added; blá-ma yid-la .p'rén-baí (bla ma yid la 'phreng ba'i) rtags, bú-mo séms-la .p'rén-bas (bu mo sems la 'phreng bas) Glr.; yáb-kyi t'úgs-la .p'rén-bar gyúr-te (yab kyi thugs la 'phreng bar gyur te), or .p'réń-bźin-du ('phreng bzhin du) as she was very dear to her father Glr.; śin-tu .p'róń-ba żig byuń (shin tu 'phrong ba zhig byung) an ardent longing for home came over me Mil. nt.

pf. .p'ros ('phros), prop. vb. n. to spró-ba (spro ba), 1. gen. with las (las), from, to proceed, issue, emanate from, to spread, in most cases rel. to rays of light; sku .od-zér .p'ró-ba (sku 'od zer 'phro ba) a body from which rays of light proceed, a body sending forth light Glr.; Cs. also relative to odours, fame etc.; occasionally in reference to descent or parentage Thgy. — 2. to proceed, to go on, continue, and .p'ro ('phro) continuation, opp. to being finished, at an end (Sch. incorr.: 'the end'); *láb-to źen-ghyi čé'-pa* (lab tho gzhan gyi chad pa) C., Schr.: the interruption of a conversation by another person; jíg-pro bčad ('jig phro bcad) the process of destruction came to an end Glr.; sbyín-pai .p'ro čád kyań slóń-mo-pai .p'ro ma čad (sbyin pa'i 'phro chad kyang slong mo pa'i 'phro ma chad) Pth. the gifts had come to an end, but not the begging; .par-.p'ro čad ('phar 'phro chad) the pulse no longer beats Thgr.; čos-bsgyúr-.p'ro-rnams bskyur (chos bsgyur 'phro rnams bskyur) the continuations of translating were thrown aside Glr.; of the soul: yód-.p'ro-la mi yoń (yod 'phro la mi yong) whilst it is still existing, it does not come forth, i.e. it vanishes imperceptibly, as soon as an attempt is made to find out its seat and to demonstrate its essence Mil.; .p'ro t'úd-pa ('phro mthud pa) to annex the remainder, to append the continuation; *'to źág-pa* ('phro bzhag pa) C. to lay the continuation aside; *šól-wa* (bshol ba) to put it off, both expressions implying an interruption of work; .p'ro lus soń ('phro lus song) or las soń (las song) a remainder is still left of what has not been used or consumed; *'di ghań 'ti-te* ('di gang mthud te) after this has been filled up (by pouring in the wanting quantity) C.

pf. and imp. p'rogs (phrogs), fut. dbrog (dbrog) 1. to rob, take away; to deprive of, prop. nor (nor), gos (gos), rgyál-poi lág-nas rgyál-sa (rgyal po'i lag nas rgyal sa) to deprive the king of his throne Glr.; hence rgyál-sa p'rógs-pai mi (rgyal sa phrogs pa'i mi) usurper Glr.; tsád-pas mii mtu-stóbs .p'rog (tshad pas mi'i mthu stobs 'phrog) the heat deprives a man of his strength Med.; yet also: sems-yid .p'róg-pa (sems yid 'phrog pa) to take another man's heart, to run away with his affections, to captivate him Glr.; .p'rog-byéd ('phrog byed), and also .p'róg-ma ('phrog ma) = dbań-p'yúg (dbang phyug) 1. ईश्वर i.e. Shiwa, or also Indra. 2. symb. num.: 11. — rku-.p'róg (rku 'phrog), robbery Ma., *čom-* (bcom) or *čom-tóg* (bcom thog), id., W.; *čòm-tóg táń-kan* (bcom thog gtang mkhan) robber, *wań dań čom-tóg čó-te* (dbang dang bcom thog byas te) by violence, W. — 2. to make one lose a thing, bdág-gi glań p'rogs (bdag gi glang phrogs) (by his negligence) he has made me lose my ox Dzl.; sdóm-pa .p'róg-tu byuń (sdom pa 'phrog tu byung) my vow is lost to me, i.e. the meditation I had vowed has been disturbed, thwarted Glr., to deprive a person of his power or place, to overthrow, kings, dignitaries etc. Stg., analogous to ryo-ba (ryo ba), .gul-ba ('gul ba), .k'rugs-pa ('khrugs pa). — 3. to remove, do away with, expel, demons Glr.
Glr., provinc. for .p'rań ('phrang) and .p'reń ('phreng), v. .p'reń-ba ('phreng ba).

1. vb.: pf. .p'rod ('phrod), vb. n. to spród-pa (sprod pa), to have been delivered, transmitted, lág-tu (lag tu) into the hands of a person, hence .p'rod-.dzín ('phrod 'dzin), *'t'od-zín* ('thod 'dzin) W. receipt, quittance; ńo (ngo) or ńos-.p'rod-pa (ngos 'phrod pa) to know, perceive, understand; so prob. also snyíń-la gsál-bar ma .p'rod (snying la gsal bar ma 'phrod) Schr. — 2. adj. fit, proper, suitable, agreeing with, congenial to, p'ó-bar (pho bar) agreeing with the stomach Med.; mi-.p'rod zas (mi 'phrod zas) unwholesome food Med.; mi-.p'rod-pa (mi 'phrod pa) also signifies adverse fortune, adversity C.;
